APPENDIX A — COMPLETE GPIO PIN MAP AND WIRING NOTES

This appendix provides expanded wiring guidance for each interface in the reference hardware configuration. All connections assume 3.3 V logic levels unless stated otherwise. ESP32 pins marked ADC1 are safe for analog reads during active Wi-Fi; ADC2 pins must not be used for analog reads when Wi-Fi is active.
TABLE A-I. Expanded GPIO Reference with Wiring Notes
DHT22 can run on 3.3V or 5V; check module board
Capacitive probe preferred; short wire run
Verify wet=LOW or wet=HIGH per module
INPUT_PULLUP; twisted pair for long runs
Flyback diode; fused load; mains isolation
Direction control; both pins tied together
APPENDIX B — AGRIFUSION PSEUDOCODE AND ALGORITHM DETAIL
Algorithm: AgriFusion Weighted Probability Fusion with Disagreement Damping
Input: feature_vector x (9-dimensional float array, fixed order)
Step 3: w_sum ← w_rf + w_xgb # = 1.0 if normalized
Step 4: p_fused ← (w_rf * p_rf + w_xgb * p_xgb) / w_sum
Step 7: c_raw ← max(p_fused) # highest class prob
Step 9: if not agree: c ← max(CONF_FLOOR, c_raw * DAMP_FACTOR)
Complexity: O(k) for k=3 classes; negligible vs. tree inference O(n_trees * depth).
The disagreement damp (×0.88) reduces overconfidence when RF and XGBoost disagree.
CONF_FLOOR = 0.35 ensures the UI always shows at least 35% confidence.
APPENDIX C — FULL REST API ROUTE CATALOG
POST /api/auth/signup — Register user; bcrypt password hash; return user profile
POST /api/auth/login — OAuth2 password flow; return JWT access token + expiry
GET /api/auth/me — Return current authenticated user profile
POST /api/auth/location — Update user GPS for advisory localization
POST /api/sensors — Ingest telemetry; NPK resolve; ML inference; SHAP; persist
GET /api/sensors/latest — Latest sensor reading row for a given farm_id
GET /api/predictions/latest — Latest forecast snapshot with all model outputs
POST /api/shap — Feature importance and narrative explanation strings
GET /api/dashboard — Aggregated dashboard DTO (sensors + prediction + SHAP)
GET /api/models/comparison — All model accuracy/F1 metrics from benchmark JSON
GET /api/history — Historical sensor time series with configurable window
GET /api/weather — External weather service fusion for advisory context
GET /api/advisories — Rule- and model-informed agronomic tip list
GET /api/trends — Statistical trend analysis over historical window
POST /api/camera/upload — Accept base64 image; thermal process; store; return
GET /api/camera/latest — Latest processed frame + thermal image for farm
POST /api/camera/diagnose — HSV heuristic plant health diagnosis on latest image
GET /api/camera/stream — MJPEG stream proxy or latest frame endpoint
GET /api/camera/stream/diagnosis — Stream with overlaid cached diagnosis
GET /api/camera/history — Historical camera image list with timestamps
GET /api/admin/overview — Admin dashboard aggregate across all farms
GET /api/admin/farms — List all farms with sensor health summary
DELETE /api/admin/farms/{id} — Remove farm and associated readings
GET /api/admin/users — List all users with role and last activity
GET /api/admin/predictions — Prediction history feed across all farms
GET /api/admin/models/performance — Per-model accuracy, F1, confusion matrix
GET /api/admin/shap/analysis — Mean absolute SHAP values per feature (all farms)
GET /api/admin/risk/analysis — Risk distribution charts across farms and crops
GET /api/admin/sensors/health — Sensor staleness and anomaly overview
GET /api/admin/export/data — CSV/JSON data export with date range filter
POST /api/admin/models/retrain — Trigger train_all_models.py and reload artifacts
GET /api/admin/models/versions — List model artifact file timestamps and sizes
APPENDIX D — RELATIONAL SCHEMA: COLUMN SEMANTICS
id (INTEGER PK): Auto-increment surrogate key; referenced by farms.user_id and prediction_history.user_id.
email (VARCHAR, UNIQUE): Login identifier; indexed for O(log n) lookup in OAuth2 password flow.
hashed_password (VARCHAR): bcrypt digest; never returned in API responses.
name (VARCHAR): Display name for UI and admin tables.
role (VARCHAR): 'farmer' or 'admin'; enforced in FastAPI dependency; stored as plain string for simplicity.
phone (VARCHAR, nullable): Optional farmer contact for advisory SMS integration.
latitude, longitude (FLOAT, nullable): GPS for weather API localization; treat as PII.
created_at (DATETIME): Registration timestamp; used in admin user activity reports.
farm_id (VARCHAR PK): Matches firmware FARM_ID constant; string type allows meaningful IDs.
name (VARCHAR): Human-readable farm name for admin UI.
location (VARCHAR): Free-text location string; not parsed for GPS.
farmer_phone (VARCHAR, nullable): Contact number; independent of users table to allow unregistered farms.
user_id (INTEGER, FK nullable): Links farm to a user account when registration is complete.
id (INTEGER PK): Auto-increment; high volume (30-second intervals per farm).
farm_id (VARCHAR): Denormalized for fast per-farm queries without JOIN.
crop (VARCHAR): Crop name at time of reading; used for NPK fallback lookup.
temperature, humidity, ph, rainfall, soil_moisture, light_intensity (FLOAT): Core sensor scalars.
water_level, water_flow, npk_n, npk_p, npk_k (FLOAT, nullable): Optional extended sensors.
confidence (FLOAT): AgriFusion confidence after optional disagreement damping.
shap_values (TEXT): JSON-serialized dict of 9 feature SHAP attributions.
timestamp (DATETIME): Server-assigned ingestion timestamp; device timestamp optional.
APPENDIX E — DEPLOYMENT AND CONFIGURATION CHECKLIST
Step 1. Clone repository from version control.
Step 2. Create Python 3.10+ virtual environment; pip install -r backend/requirements.txt.
Step 3. Copy config/env.example to .env; set DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET (32+ random bytes), and optional ESP32_CAMERA_IP.
Step 4. Run python backend/train_all_models.py; verify fusion_metrics.json and all .pkl files are generated.
Step 5. Start FastAPI: uvicorn backend.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000; confirm /health returns 200.
Step 7. Run npm install (or pnpm install) in project root.
Step 8. Run npm run dev for development or npm run build && npm run start for production.
Step 9. Configure firmware: set WIFI_SSID, WIFI_PASSWORD, API_URL to http://<server>:8000/api/sensors.
Step 10. Set FARM_ID, CROP_NAME, and calibration constants (SOIL_DRY_ADC, SOIL_WET_ADC, FLOW_PULSES_PER_LITER, WIND_KMH_PER_HZ).
Step 11. Flash firmware to ESP32; monitor Serial at 115200 baud for successful POST confirmation.
Step 12. Create admin user via /api/auth/signup with role='admin' or via database seed script.
Step 13. Login to web UI; navigate to admin dashboard; confirm sensor readings appear.
Step 14. Run soil moisture dry/wet calibration; update SOIL_DRY_ADC and SOIL_WET_ADC; reflash.
Step 15. Perform pH buffer calibration; update firmware pH mapping constants.
Step 16. Bucket-test flow meter; adjust FLOW_PULSES_PER_LITER to match measured volume.
Step 17. Field calibrate wind anemometer against reference if available.
Step 18. Verify Modbus NPK reads against probe manual; adjust scaling factor.
Step 19. Configure reverse proxy with TLS for production; update NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL to https://.
Step 20. Schedule daily database backup via cron or cloud snapshot.
Step 21. Pin Python and npm dependency versions; document in requirements.txt and package.json.
Step 22. Train operators on relay interlock procedures and emergency manual disconnect.
APPENDIX F — CROP NPK REFERENCE TABLE (FIRMWARE FALLBACK VALUES)
TABLE F-I. Crop-Table NPK Default Values (Backend Fallback when Modbus Fails)
ADC: Analog-to-digital converter, 12-bit SAR on ESP32.
AgriFusion: Weighted softmax probability fusion layer combining RF and XGBoost outputs with disagreement damping.
API: Application programming interface; REST/JSON in AgriXplain.
ASGI: Asynchronous server gateway interface; used by FastAPI and Uvicorn.
Bayesian Ridge: Regularized linear regression with Bayesian weight estimation; used for uncertainty-style outputs.
BNC: Bayonet Neill-Concelman connector used on pH electrode modules.
BOM: Bill of materials listing hardware components and costs.
Capacitor: Ionic framework that wraps web apps as native Android/iOS applications.
CORS: Cross-origin resource sharing; HTTP header policy for browser security.
DHT22: Digital temperature and relative humidity sensor (AM2302).
ESP32: Espressif dual-core Wi-Fi/Bluetooth SoC used as edge MCU.
FastAPI: Python ASGI web framework with automatic OpenAPI documentation.
GPIO: General-purpose input/output pin on the ESP32.
HTTP: Hypertext transfer protocol; used for device-to-backend and client-to-backend communication.
I2C: Inter-integrated circuit; two-wire serial bus (SDA/SCL) for LCD.
ISR: Interrupt service routine; handles GPIO pulse counting for flow and wind.
JSON: JavaScript object notation; payload format for telemetry and API responses.
JWT: JSON web token; signed bearer token for authentication.
LDR: Light-dependent resistor; used as light intensity proxy.
LCD: Liquid crystal display; 16x2 character display with I2C backpack.
Modbus RTU: Serial fieldbus protocol; RS485 physical layer; used for NPK probe communication.
Next.js: React meta-framework for web applications; used for dashboards.
NPK: Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), Potassium (K); primary macronutrients in soil.
ORM: Object-relational mapper; SQLAlchemy in AgriXplain.
OV2640: CMOS image sensor used in ESP32-CAM modules.
PAR: Photosynthetically active radiation; spectral range 400-700 nm relevant to plant growth.
pH: Measure of hydrogen ion concentration; soil acidity/alkalinity indicator.
Pydantic: Python data validation library used for request/response models in FastAPI.
REST: Representational state transfer; HTTP architectural style for the AgriXplain API.
RF: Random Forest; bagged ensemble of decision trees.
RS485: Differential serial communication standard; physical layer for Modbus.
SHAP: SHapley Additive exPlanations; game-theoretic framework for model feature attribution.
SQL: Structured query language; used with SQLite or PostgreSQL via SQLAlchemy.
UART: Universal asynchronous receiver-transmitter; used for RS485 and debug serial.
XGBoost: Extreme gradient boosting; tree ensemble classifier.
